seo.title: Clinical Evidence for Able Assess | Able Care
seo.description: Peer-reviewed research linking grip strength and functional health to falls, frailty, mortality and clinical outcomes. The evidence base behind Able Assess.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Therapist conducting clinical assessment with patient
blocks.headline: The science behind Able Assess.
blocks.subtitle: Peer-reviewed research linking grip strength and functional health to falls, frailty, mortality and clinical outcomes. Every claim on this site is grounded in the evidence below.

blocks.heading: Grip strength and mortality
blocks.pulledStat: Grip strength predicts all-cause mortality more reliably than systolic blood pressure.

citations.title: Prognostic value of grip strength: findings from the Prospective Urban Rural Epidemiology (PURE) study
Association between muscular strength and mortality in men: prospective cohort study
Grip strength as a predictor of all-cause mortality: a systematic review and meta-analysis

blocks.heading: Falls prevention and functional assessment
blocks.pulledStat: Multifactorial falls risk screening outperforms single-test approaches in predicting falls in older adults.

citations.title: Effectiveness of multifactorial interventions in preventing falls among older adults in the community
Walking speed: the sixth vital sign
Falls in older people: assessing risk and prevention

blocks.heading: Grip strength in clinical populations
blocks.pulledStat: Low grip strength is associated with increased risk of cardiovascular disease, respiratory disease, renal disease and cancer mortality.

citations.title: Grip strength and cardiovascular risk markers: a systematic review
Handgrip strength and health outcomes: Umbrella review of systematic reviews with meta-analyses of observational studies
citations.journal: Journal of Sport and Health Science, 2021

citations.title: Changes in lean body mass with glucagon-like peptide-1-based therapies and mitigation strategies

blocks.heading: Four validated metrics in one workflow

metrics.name: Grip strength
metrics.whatItMeasures: Upper-body strength proxy. Predicts all-cause mortality, frailty and hospitalization risk.

metrics.name: Sit-to-stand
metrics.whatItMeasures: Lower-limb power and balance. A key indicator of fall likelihood and functional decline.

metrics.name: Gait speed
metrics.whatItMeasures: The 'sixth vital sign' for older adults. Linked to rehospitalization, disability and mortality.

metrics.name: Timed Up and Go
metrics.whatItMeasures: Falls risk assessment in under a minute. Validated across home, community and clinical settings.

blocks.heading: Talk to the clinical team about the evidence.
blocks.bodyText: Our clinical team can walk you through the research and how it applies to your setting.

blocks.heading: Grip Strength: The Essential Biomarker for Longevity
blocks.promoBody: Download the full clinical summary — peer-reviewed evidence linking grip strength to biological age, mortality, frailty and chronic disease risk, with guidance on integrating grip testing into routine assessment.
